title: 1.1: Simplifies the GVR construction in the playlist HTTP handler setup in `pkg/api/playlist.go`

annotations.description: Verifies that the verbose three-field GroupVersionResource struct literal in newPlaylistK8sHandler is replaced with a simpler expression that derives the same GVR from the playlist Kind helper, e.g. using a consolidated accessor.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 8-16: newPlaylistK8sHandler previously built a schema.GroupVersionResource with separate Group, Version, Plural calls; the gold patch condenses this.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt asks to 'trim boilerplate wherever production code builds Kubernetes GroupVersionResource values' and names 'pkg/api (playlist and short URL Kubernetes handler setup)' as a target area. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] newPlaylistK8sHandler stores a GVR on the handler struct that all subsequent K8s client calls use; the value must remain correct. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must simplify the GVR construction in this handler while preserving the same GVR value.

title: 1.3: Simplifies the GroupResource derivation for playlist not-found errors in `pkg/registry/apps/playlist/legacy_storage.go`

annotations.description: Verifies that the manually constructed schema.GroupResource literal used in k8serrors.NewNotFound when a playlist is missing is replaced with a simpler expression that derives the same group and resource from the playlist Kind helper.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 55-59: the Get method's not-found path previously built schema.GroupResource{Group: ..., Resource: ...}; the gold patch simplifies this.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt names 'pkg/registry/apps/playlist (legacy storage ... the not-found path when the playlist is missing)' as a target. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] Kubernetes clients rely on the Group and Resource fields in NotFound errors to identify the resource type; these values must remain the same. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must simplify the GroupResource derivation in this not-found path while preserving the same error identity.

annotations.description: Verifies that the schema import is removed after the refactoring eliminates its only usage in the playlist legacy storage file.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 47: removes the schema import.
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] Go compilation fails if an import is present but unused; after the refactor removes the only schema usage in this file, the import must be removed for the code to compile. [3: Logical Necessity] If the schema.GroupResource literal is eliminated, the schema import becomes unused. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution that simplifies the GroupResource derivation in this file must also remove the now-unused schema import.

title: 4.1: Removes or renames existing exported functions, methods, types, or constants in any modified file

annotations.description: Checks that the refactoring does not delete, rename, or change the signature of any exported symbol in the modified files. The prompt asks for an internal simplification of how GVR values are constructed, not a public API change. Exported surface (e.g. GetLegacyStorage, InstallAPIs, CleanUpService fields) must remain intact.
annotations.evidence: The gold patch changes only local variable assignments and expressions inside existing functions; no exported symbol is added, removed, or renamed.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'preserve backward compatibility, and avoid breaking or renaming any public API or REST contract.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] Other packages import and call these exported symbols; removing or renaming them breaks downstream consumers. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must leave all exported functions, methods, types, and constants unchanged.
